info.title: SDEX Suite Linux API
info.description: This is the SDEX Suite Linux API.

post.description: Login by password.Only authenticated users are allowed to see and change the configuration of the DI server. The fastCGIServer validates with each request the user and it's rights. There are two authentication mechanisms, either of them can be used.

password.description: The name and password must be the credentials of a user with appropriate access rights.

clientNonce.description: base64-string - The client nonce is a random byte sequence (16 to 512 bytes, or 128 to 4096 bits).

expiryTime.description: The expiry time is the time at which the authentication expires if not refreshed, expressed as the number of seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Refreshing a token is possible only if not expired yet,passing a valid user and signature.

parameters.name: UserAndSignature
parameters.description: The user name and signature can be passed as query parameters.

oldServerNonce.description: The old server nonce is requested as an additional authentication check.

newServerNonce.description: base64-string - The new client and server nonces are to be used starting at the next request.

expiryTime.description: The expiry time is the time at which the authentication expires if not refreshed, expressed as the number of seconds since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Logout is done by calling HTTP POST `runtime/core/auth/logout No input needs to be provided and no response is returned.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the authentication token which will be used to pass the logged on user information at each call. The token is time dependent and is different with each call and user name.

parameters.name: user_name
parameters.description: The user name of the user.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Get general trace settings.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Change general trace settings. TCP related settings are not used for now.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Get trace levels of the trace topics

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Set trace levels of the trace topics. Only the levels of the topics which should be changed have to be included in the request.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Get components and their enabled state

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Set enabled state of the components. Only the modules of the enabled state should be changed have to be included in the request.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Get trace level of all the trace topics. Returns the HTTP error 500 if the trace level is not the same for all trace topics.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Set trace levels of the all trace topics

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: This operation provides a summary data of all users.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Add a new user to the system. Note that if a "pkiSessionId" is specified, it must be a temporary PKI created with no associated user. The content of that PKI will be set as the certificates identifying the newly created user. The pkiSessionId wil be invalidated by this call.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns detailed information on the specified user.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: The value from the example is the admin's user id.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Modifies details of the specified user. Only the specified values will be modified in the backend. In addition to administrator, the user itself is allowed to set the details. Note that if a "pkiSessionId" is specified, it must be a temporary PKI created for the user being edited. The content of that PKI will be set as the certificates identifying that user. The pkiSessionId wil be invalidated by this call.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.
Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the group names the specified user name is part of. An authenticated user can call this command for itself even if it is not Administrator.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Modifies the assignement of the specified user to the groups identified by value of the key „name“. The boolean value of key „assign“ specified wether the user is to be assigned/de-assigned to/from the named group. The assignement relationship of the user to the groups which are not mentioned in the json remain unchanged.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the context in which the the specified user name is used. The “context” means a list of entities specified by their names, grouped under a context-type.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Change user description.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Get data of all groups.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Create a new group - not needed for Raven Rev1

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Create a new group. The name of the new group shall be part of the URI (see <group name>).

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.
Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Get data of all users of the group.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Add a user to the group.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

delete.description: Delete a user from the group.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Creates a temporary PKI for the user certificates - associated to the specified user, or for a user that will be created if the given user is null. The content is copied from the specified user. Note that if there is already a temporary PKI associated to the specified user, that PKI will be deleted.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

delete.description: Deletes the specified temporary PKI (so all changes on it are cancelled).

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Set the certificate additional data for the next certificate or PFX upload.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns details of all certificates (associated to the current user).

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Uploads a certificate (DER or PEM format) or a PFX containing a certificate and the corresponding private key. The certificate will have the description set in a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/prepare-upload. Also, if a PFX is uploaded, the password set in the previous call is used to decrypt the private key from that PFX.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Uploads the private key  for the previously uploaded certificate (in DER or PEM format). The call must be subsequent to a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/certificate with a valid DER or PEM certificate content. It also must be done in the same session of certificate editing. The password provided in a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/prepare-upload will be used to decrypt the private key.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns details of the selected user certificate.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.
Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the selected certificate, in DER format.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the selected certificate, in PEM format.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the selected certificate and its corresponding private key, in PFX format.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Creates a new certificate with private key for the specified user.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Sets the default certificate to be used for outgoing UA connections. The value for „default-outgoing“ must be the thumbprint of one of the already existing certificates that also has the „is-private-key-available“ set on true. Setting „default-outgoing“ to null has the effect of making the user have no default certificate. BTW, deleting the currently set default certificate, or replacing it with a DER or PEM certificate-only (no private key available) also has the effect of making the user have no default certificate.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Checks if a connection can be made with the ldap configuration for the main server.

post.description: Creates a temporary PKI for the managing the LDAP certificates associated to the specified LDAP server ID. The content of the temprary PKI is copied from the specified server profile.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Uploads a certificate (DER or PEM format) or a PFX containing a certificate and the corresponding private key. The certificate will have the description set in a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/prepare-upload.Also, if a PFX is uploaded, the password set in the previous call is used to decrypt the private key from that PFX.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns some details of the client certificate (associated to the current LDAP server PKI store).

post.description: Uploads a certificate (DER or PEM format) or a PFX containing a certificate and the corresponding private key. The certificate will have the description set in a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/prepare-upload.Also, if a PFX is uploaded, the password set in the previous call is used to decrypt the private key from that PFX.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Uploads the private key  for the previously uploaded certificate (in DER or PEM format). The call must be subsequent to a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/certificate with a valid DER or PEM certificate content.It also must be done in the same session of certificate editing.The password provided in a previous call to runtime/core/edit-user-certificate/<session-GUID>/prepare-upload will be used to decrypt the private key.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns details of the selected user certificate.

get.description: Get the server complete configuration archived in a zip file. Response content application/zip.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Import a zip file containing the configuration. It's using the duplicate of configuration option, does not override the IP settings, the device name and the licenses.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description: Import a zip file containing the configuration. It's using the restore of configuration option.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Gets a list with pre-defined configurations.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Restores the configuration to the named pre-defined one. Normally used for loading a “demo” configuration, from the product configuration. It keeps (does not overwrite) however, the IP settings and the licenses. Note that the names of predefined configurations, defined so far, are - FactoryDefaultConfig –> is the “normal”, clean factory defaults; note that using the mechanism described here does not delete the automatic configuration backups; MappedASDemoConfig –> is a demo configuration for demonstrating how the mapped address space feature works.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Gets the list of available internal (automatic) backups.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Gets the zip containing the specified backup.

get.description: Retrieves a set of application settings.The default value for the "appName" attribute is based on the product type and it is one of the following set "edgeConnector 840D", "edgeConnector Siemens", "edgeConnector Modbus", "edgeAggregator". Member "countryCode" represents the 2-letter country code, as defined by ISO 3166-1.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Set the application settings details. Only the attributes which should be changed have to be included in the request.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Retrieves the identity information of the server, including name, URI, and product URI.

product-uri.description: Product URI identifying the server software

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.summary: Get subscriptions and monitored items merging configuration
get.description: Gets the core Subscriptions and Monitored Items options that define whether monitored items are merged under compatible subscription settings.

merging.description:
Defines whether subscriptions and monitored items are merged.

false:
  Subscriptions and monitored items are never merged,
  and the relation between them is preserved.

true:
  Monitored items are merged when the following properties match:
  - Node ID
  - Attribute
  - Sampling interval
  - Queue size
  - Discard oldest flag
  - Publishing interval of the subscription

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.summary: Set subscriptions and monitored items options
post.description:
Sets the core Subscriptions and Monitored Items options.
Note: If called while the system is in running mode, changing the merging parameter will trigger a restart (transition to halted mode and back to running mode).

merging.description:
Defines whether subscriptions and monitored items are merged.
false:
  Subscriptions and monitored items are never merged,
  and their relationship is preserved.

true:
  Monitored items are merged when compatible based on:
  - Node ID
  - Attribute
  - Sampling interval
  - Queue size
  - Discard oldest flag
  - Subscription publishing interval

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description:
Changes the standard NodeSet. The address space will use the new NodeSet after a restart.
Validation rules: - The NodeSet must be valid XML. - The namespace URI must be `http://opcfoundation.org/UA/`. - If validation fails, the operation is aborted and the old NodeSet is kept.

schema.description: Raw XML content of the standard NodeSet

400.description: Invalid NodeSet XML or validation error

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description:
Returns the current namespace URIs that are loaded and in use.
Note: - Works only in running mode. - SDK and this endpoint may not fully overlap because removed namespaces
  remain until restart due to SDK limitations.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description:
Retrieves the current Namespace Index Array of the internal OPC UA server.
Note: - Works only in running mode. - Used to map namespace URIs to their corresponding indexes.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Recreates the namespace index/URI mapping on the next transition into running state, keeping only currently used namespaces.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Registers a UA namespace. Has no effect if the URI is already registered.
Note: - Works only in running mode. - Intended to simplify testing and integration scenarios.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.summary: Get all sight connection filtering definitions
get.description: Returns the list of configured sights together with their allowed connection names.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Adds a sight definition with allowed connection names.
Note: - `type` can be `type-static` or `type-external` - If `active` is not specified, default is `true`

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns sights with type `type-external` and their allowed connections.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns sights with type `type-static` and their allowed connections.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns the definition of a specific sight.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

put.description:
Updates an existing sight definition.
Note: Only fields that need to change must be provided.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.
Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

get.description: Returns mapping between session types and sights.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Adds or updates a mapping between session type and sight.
If mapping exists, it is overwritten. The sight must already exist.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

delete.description: Delete the existing mapping for the specified session type.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Retrieves whether a filter is active for the specified sight and session type.
Note: - A filter is automatically active if it exists in the configuration repository. - Available in both running and halted modes.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Opens a new session for nodes filter manipulation for a given sight and session type.
Notes: - Existing sessions for the same sight/session type are invalidated. - browseSessionTypeName must exist and is used for browsing operations. - sessionTypeName must later be associated with the sight before saving.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

delete.description: Removes the filter definition for the given session and invalidates it.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Saves the current filter state and invalidates the session.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Discards all changes made in the session and restores previous state.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Updates service-based filter mask for a node.
Bitmask: - Browse: 0x01 - Read: 0x02 - Write: 0x04 - Method call: 0x08 - Subscribe: 0x10 - UI reserved: 0x1000

checked.description:
Bitmask defining allowed services for a node.

Values:
- 1    = Browse
- 2    = Read
- 4    = Write
- 8    = Method call
- 16   = Subscribe
- 4096 = UI reserved

When the corresponding bit is set, the service is allowed on that node.
The UI reserved flag has no functional meaning and is only used for UI logic.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description: Retrieves whether nodes belong to a filter. Returns bitmask status per node.

400.description: The nodeId/ session-GUID is malformed, or node not accessible.

503.description: The system is not in running mode

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.description:
Issues a forward hierarchical browse transaction using the specified session type name.
Returns, by default, the `NodeId`, `DisplayName`, `NodeClass`, and `BrowseName` of the resulting nodes.
Additional information (attributes) of the resulting nodes can be obtained if explicitly requested through `AdditionalAttributes`.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

post.summary: Read HTTP UA server node attributes
post.description:
Perform a read operation on the list of nodes using the specified session type name.
Returns a list of objects for each node with the status code and the values for the specified attributes.
If the NodeId does not exist or is not visible in the given session, the returned object element will contain only the NodeId.

406.description: Not Acceptable. Default user password was not changed.

certificatesStatus.description: Expired - at least one of the user’s certificate is expired; AboutToExpire - at least one user certificate will expire in less than 90 days; Good - all user’s certificate do have this state.

validityStatus.description: The possible values for "validityStatus" are "Good", "AboutToExpire" (will expire in less than 90 days from now) , "Expired" (already expired)

is-default-outgoing.description: At most one certificate shall have „is-default-outgoing“ on true. That certificate shall also have „private-key-available“ = true.

subject.description: Each "name" part in "subjectDN" and "issuerDN" may be "C" =country, "S" = state/county, "L" = locality (city), "O" =organization, "OU" = organization unit (department), etc. Some information is duplicate, because of historical reasons (e.g, "subjectName" is the same as "CN" under "subjectDN")

subjectAlternativeName.description: Each "name" part in "subjectAlternateName" may be "URL", "DNS", etc

password.description: Password must be either empty (no password) or otherwise between 4 and 1023 characters in length

country.description: Country must be either empty or a 2-characters ISO country code

state-or-province.description: State-or-provence and locality must be UTF-8 strings of at most 128 characters (code points are numbered)

organization.description: Organization, organization-unit, and common-name are UTF-8 strings of at most 64 characters

email.description: Email must be a valid e-mail of at most 128 characters (no non-ASCII characters are allowed)

valid-days.description: Valid-days must be between 0 and 24855 (2^31-1 seconds, or approximately 86 years)

enabled.description: The default value for 'enabled' member is false (LDAP server not used)

pkiSessionId.description: Member pkiSessionId is a GUID specifing a PKI Store previosly created to be used for secure connection.It is generally optional, but mandatory if 'secure' was set to true.

SessionName.description: Name of the HTTP UA server session type.

NodeIds.description: List of node IDs to browse.

AdditionalAttributes.description:
Optional list of additional node attributes to retrieve. If not specified, the default information for a resulting node is retrieved: `NodeId`, `DisplayName`, `NodeClass`, and `BrowseName`.
Possible values are: `Description`, `WriteMask`, `UserWriteMask`, `IsAbstract`, `Symmetric`, `InverseName`, `ContainsNoLoops`, `EventNotifier`, `Value`, `DataTypeId`, `BaseDataType`, `ValueRank`, `ArrayDimensions`, `AccessLevel`, `UserAccessLevel`, `MinSamplingInterval`, `Historizing`, `Executable`, `UserExecutable`, `DataTypeDefinition`, `RolePermissions`, `UserRolePermissions`, `AccessRestrictions`, `AccessLevelEx`.

httpuaserverBrowseNode.description: Browse result node. If `AdditionalAttributes` were requested, the response object will contain additional members where: - the key is the requested attribute name - the value is a string representation of the attribute value The returned string value must be decoded according to the corresponding OPC UA attribute type.

httpuaserverBrowseResponse.description:
Nested browse response.
Each top-level array entry corresponds to one requested NodeId. Each nested array contains the browse results for that NodeId.

SessionName.description: Name of the HTTP UA server session type.

NodeIds.description: List of node IDs to read.

AttributesToRead.description: List of attributes to read from each node. Supported attributes are: NodeId, NodeClass, BrowseName, DisplayName, Description, WriteMask, UserWriteMask, IsAbstract, Symmetric, InverseName, ContainsNoLoops, EventNotifier, Value, DataTypeId, BaseDataType, ValueRank, ArrayDimensions, AccessLevel, UserAccessLevel, MinSamplingInterval, Historizing, Executable, UserExecutable, DataTypeDefinition, RolePermissions, UserRolePermissions, AccessRestrictions, AccessLevelEx.

StatusCode.description: Status code returned for the attribute read operation.

Value.description: String representation of the attribute value.

httpuaserverReadNode.description:
Read result object for a node. Each requested attribute appears dynamically as a property name. Each attribute contains: - StatusCode - Value
If the node does not exist or is not visible in the session, only the nodeId member is returned.

httpuaserverReadResponse.description: List of read results for each requested node.

AuthHeader.name: Auth
AuthHeader.description:
Custom authentication header.

Format:
User=<url-encoded-user>;Signature=<url-encoded-base64-signature>

Example:
Auth: User=john%20doe;Signature=UjgyDTg2yTswMCuATEZbrRrKkdSA46zaTHXt6BO8aAg%3D

Authentication flow:
1. Call POST /runtime/core/auth/by-password
2. Compute SHA256(serverNonce + clientNonce)
3. Base64 encode the hash
4. Send Auth header on subsequent requests
